Title: A transcript profiling approach reveals an epicatechin-specific glucosyltransferase expressed in the seed coat of Medicago truncatula
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Double CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) used to express Arabidopsis TT2 in Medicago hairy roots
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use TT2-based activation as a regulatory entry point to induce the PA program in legumes, but deploy with caution because ectopic TF expression can cause pleiotropic/off-target transcription. Practical value lies in stacking TT2 with missing transport/assembly genes or tissue-specific seed/forage promoters to engineer condensed tannins in forage legumes such as alfalfa for bloat protection and improved nitrogen utilization.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ectopic TT2 expression triggered massive accumulation of soluble and especially insoluble proanthocyanidins in Medicago hairy roots, with accompanying increases in anthocyanins and flavonols.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Hairy-root transgenic overexpression; DMACA staining; normal-phase HPLC with postcolumn DMACA derivatization for soluble PAs; butanol-HCl hydrolysis and HPLC of insoluble PAs; microarray profiling; RT-PCR.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hairy roots cultured on B5 agar with kanamycin under fluorescent light, 16-h photoperiod; developing seed-coat analysis across 10-36 days after pollination, especially 12-24 dap.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Arabidopsis TT2 transgene was ectopically expressed in Medicago hairy roots; endogenous PA pathway activation compared with natural PA biosynthesis in developing seed coat.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In Arabidopsis, TT2 is a key determinant for proanthocyanidin accumulation in developing seed coat.
Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not reported in this paper for TT2 ORF
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix microarray transcript profiling of TT2-expressing vs control hairy roots; Affymetrix microarray of seed coat-preferential expression; Medicago Gene Expression Atlas mining (Benedito et al. 2008).
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: TT2 was the central experimental trigger used to identify downstream PA-associated genes, notably UGT72L1. The paper emphasizes that ectopic high-level TF expression can create artifactual pleiotropic effects, so seed-coat expression filtering was added to prioritize genuine PA-related targets.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential off-target transactivation and abnormal metabolic load; no obvious visible growth phenotype in hairy roots was reported.
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus (expected for MYB TF; not directly tested here)
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Arabidopsis TT2 expression in Medicago hairy roots strongly induced PA accumulation, especially insoluble polymers. 2. TT2 up-regulated known flavonoid/PA biosynthetic genes and helped identify seed-coat PA candidates, including UGT72L1. 3. TT2 therefore serves as a functional switch to activate the PA metabolic program in a legume context.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat naturally; transgenic hairy roots upon TT2 expression
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective plant polyphenols; in forage crops reduce pasture bloat, methane generation, urinary nitrogen excretion, and help counter intestinal parasites; antioxidant and human health-promoting activities.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native expression in seed coat; indirectly induced in hairy roots by ectopic TT2 under double CaMV 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate engineering gene for modulating PA precursor glucosylation in legumes. Best deployed with pathway and transport components rather than alone, and likely with seed coat or forage tissue-specific promoters. Knockout may not reduce total PA level and may instead alter PA structure or precursor homeostasis, so application should focus on tuning PA composition/transport rather than simply increasing content.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to (-)-epicatechin, forming epicatechin 3'-O-glucoside.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: UGT72L1 expression correlates with epicatechin glucoside accumulation in developing seed coat and with PA biosynthetic activity; enzyme is highly specific for the PA precursor (-)-epicatechin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Microarray coexpression with TT2 induction and seed coat preference; RT-PCR cloning; recombinant MBP-fusion expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assays with substrate panel; LC-MS/UV; beta-glucosidase hydrolysis; NMR structural identification of product; developmental metabolite profiling in seeds.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a modified precursor likely used for transport, storage, regulation, or correct incorporation into PA polymerization rather than directly forming the final PA polymer.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seed coat, especially around 10-24 dap; detected in TT2-expressing hairy roots under tissue culture conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly seed-coat preferential; >10-fold higher in seed coat than other organs; developmental transcript peak parallels ANR and CHS, highest in early-to-mid developing seeds; induced 64.8-fold by TT2 in hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species because gene is from Medicago truncatula.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Not experimentally validated; proposed functional relationship with TT12 transporter and an induced beta-glucosidase
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Side-route / precursor modification step upstream of vacuolar transport/polymerization
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix microarray transcript profiling; Medicago Gene Expression Atlas mining; LC-MS metabolite profiling.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Protein is 482 aa, apparently intronless in BAC AC124966, likely present in three genomic copies by DNA gel blot. Highly specific for 2,3-cis-flavan-3-ol stereochemistry: best substrate (-)-epicatechin, moderate activity with (-)-epigallocatechin, weak with (+)-catechin and cyanidin, inactive with procyanidins B1/B2 and many other flavonoids/isoflavonoids. Product identified as epicatechin 3'-O-glucoside. Preliminary transposon insertion mutants showed unaltered or increased PA levels, suggesting a role in transport, detoxification, starter/extension unit balance, or linkage control rather than absolute PA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Proanthocyanidin precursor decoration / epicatechin glucosylation branch
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may alter PA structure, linkage type, precursor pools, or transport without necessarily increasing total PA. Knockout may not reduce PAs and could increase them.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Proposed key step in PA biosynthesis or regulation, but not established as rate-limiting; likely a specialized control/handling step for epicatechin precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT72L1 was one of the strongest TT2-induced and seed-coat-enriched genes associated with PA accumulation. 2. Recombinant UGT72L1 specifically glucosylated (-)-epicatechin to epicatechin 3'-O-glucoside. 3. Epicatechin glucoside was detected in developing seed and declined as insoluble PAs accumulated, supporting a precursor-handling/regulatory role in PA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant defense/protection; beneficial forage trait improving protein utilization and reducing bloat/methane; antioxidant and human health benefits.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native expression; induced in hairy roots via ectopic TT2 under double CaMV 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for PA engineering because it sits directly on the epicatechin-forming branch. Useful for boosting PA biosynthesis when combined with upstream flux and regulatory genes such as TT2; however, proper transport/polymerization capacity may also be required.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ANR was the most strongly induced gene after TT2 expression and marks activation of the epicatechin/PA branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-PCR detection in TT2-positive hairy roots; Affymetrix microarray showing 473.3-fold induction; correlation of developmental expression with PA deposition.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the direct monomeric precursor of proanthocyanidins.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seed coat during early seed development; induced in TT2-expressing hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in developing seed coat, peaking around 10-12 dap then declining by 36 dap; absent/low in control roots but strongly induced in TT2-expressing hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Functionally downstream of TT2; Arabidopsis TT2/TT8/TTG1 complex known to activate orthologous pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream branch step toward PA monomer formation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix microarray; RT-PCR; Medicago Gene Expression Atlas mining.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously known PA biosynthetic gene; used here as a benchmark validating TT2 activation of PA biosynthesis. Epicatechin-derived cyanidin was released from insoluble PA hydrolysis, supporting this branch activity.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert anthocyanidin intermediates away from anthocyanins depending on pathway context, though both anthocyanins and PAs increased here.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key committed branch step for PA monomer formation; strongly supported but not explicitly called rate-limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANR transcripts were undetectable in control hairy roots but abundant in TT2-expressing roots. 2. Microarray identified ANR as the most strongly induced TT2 target. 3. Its developmental expression in seed coat parallels PA accumulation, consistent with a direct role in epicatechin-derived PA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective condensed tannins with forage and health benefits.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native expression; induced in hairy roots via ectopic TT2 under double CaMV 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be stacked with ANR and TT2 to increase flavan-3-ol precursor supply, but because Medicago seed-coat PAs are primarily epicatechin-based, LAR may more strongly affect catechin-related composition than bulk native PA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: TT2 up-regulated leucoanthocyanidin reductase transcripts, indicating broader activation of flavan-3-ol biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Affymetrix microarray expression profiling referenced in Table S1.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms a flavan-3-ol precursor that can contribute as starter or extender units to PAs, although Medicago PAs are primarily epicatechin-based.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed coat developmental PA accumulation context and TT2-expressing hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: TT2-induced in hairy roots; implicated in developing seed coat PA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream branch step in flavan-3-ol formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper notes that Medicago seed-coat PAs are comprised primarily of (-)-epicatechin units, so LAR may play a less dominant role than ANR in this species/context.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could alter PA monomer composition and polymer properties if overexpressed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LAR transcripts were up-regulated by TT2. 2. This supports activation of a broader flavan-3-ol network in transgenic hairy roots. 3. Because native Medicago PAs are mainly epicatechin-based, LAR likely influences composition more than core flux.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective condensed tannins with forage and health benefits.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native expression; induced in hairy roots via ectopic TT2 under double CaMV 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for increasing precursor supply into both anthocyanin and PA branches; would be most effective when coupled with branch-selecting regulators such as TT2 and downstream enzymes such as ANR.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes leucoanthocyanidins to anthocyanidins such as cyanidin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Anthocyanidin synthase transcripts were induced by TT2, consistent with increased anthocyanins and PA precursor flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Affymetrix microarray expression profiling referenced in Table S1; metabolite analysis of anthocyanin increase in TT2 roots.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate anthocyanidin precursors for ANR-mediated epicatechin synthesis and anthocyanin production.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed coat developmental PA context; TT2-expressing hairy roots under culture conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Up-regulated by TT2 in hairy roots; part of seed-coat PA gene expression program.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Mid-to-downstream step feeding PA and anthocyanin branches
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Increased cyanidin release after butanol-HCl hydrolysis of insoluble PA fraction supports strong flux through cyanidin/epicatechin-related intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Common late flavonoid pathway prior to anthocyanin/PA branch partitioning
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could also enhance anthocyanin accumulation rather than exclusively PAs.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ANS transcripts were induced by TT2. 2. This supports increased flux into anthocyanidins that feed both anthocyanin and PA synthesis. 3. The metabolite phenotype of TT2 roots is consistent with this activated late flavonoid step.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective condensed tannins with forage and health benefits.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native expression; induced in hairy roots via ectopic TT2 under double CaMV 35S promoter
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to enhance flavonol accumulation, but in PA engineering may represent competing or parallel flux; tuning rather than simple overexpression may be preferable if PA yield is the priority.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroflavonols to flavonols such as quercetin.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: FLS transcripts were induced 16.6-fold by TT2, and quercetin/flavonol accumulation appeared in TT2-expressing roots but not controls.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Parallel branch from shared flavonoid precursors; reflects broader TT2-driven flavonoid activation rather than direct PA formation.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TT2-expressing hairy roots under culture conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in TT2-expressing hairy roots.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch point / side branch in flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The increase in FLS shows that TT2 ectopic expression is not absolutely restricted to PA genes in Medicago and may broaden flavonoid network activation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert shared substrates away from PA branch in some contexts, although both PAs and flavonols increased here.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FLS was induced 16.6-fold by TT2. 2. TT2 roots accumulated flavonols, notably quercetin. 3. This indicates broader flavonoid pathway activation beyond the strict PA branch.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant pigments and protective specialized metabolites.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native expression in seed coat; used as developmental comparison marker
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Classic upstream entry-point enzyme for boosting flavonoid flux; useful in stacking with TT2/ANR/UGT72L1 for PA engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses p-coumaroyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA to form chalcone scaffold for flavonoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CHS expression pattern in seed development paralleled ANR and UGT72L1, supporting coordinated PA pathway activation in seed coat.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression pattern analysis using Medicago Gene Expression Atlas/mining; prior pathway knowledge cited.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies core flavonoid precursor pool upstream of PAs.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds, especially early stages around 10-12 dap.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High during early seed development, paralleling PA deposition kinetics.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream committed entry step into flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Gene expression atlas mining / microarray-derived developmental profiles.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used here mainly as a benchmark for developmental coexpression rather than as a newly discovered gene.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broadly increases many flavonoid classes, not PA-specific.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Upstream committed step, but not specifically evaluated in this paper as rate-limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHS served as a reference PA/flavonoid biosynthetic marker in developing seed coat. 2. UGT72L1 expression kinetics paralleled CHS and ANR. 3. This coexpression supported candidacy of UGT72L1 in the PA pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protective condensed tannins with forage and health benefits.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Double CaMV 35S promoter (constitutive) used to express Arabidopsis TT2 in Medicago hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong top-level regulator for engineering PA accumulation in legumes and potentially forage crops. Best used with tissue-specific or inducible promoters and complementary transport/polymerization genes to avoid pleiotropy from constitutive overexpression.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a key positive regulator that activates endogenous flavonoid/PA genes and causes massive PA accumulation in transgenic Medicago hairy roots.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Constitutive overexpression in hairy roots; metabolite phenotyping; RT-PCR and microarray of downstream targets.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master positive switch for PA pathway activation in this experiment; sufficient to trigger strong PA accumulation and reveal downstream pathway genes.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Hairy root culture conditions; comparison with natural seed-coat developmental program.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Ectopically expressed in hairy roots from transgene.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Developing Arabidopsis seed coat PA regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not reported in this paper for TT2 ORF
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: PA biosynthesis and associated flavonoid branch activation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix microarray; developmental expression atlas comparisons.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although highly effective, TT2 overexpression also induced anthocyanin and flavonol pathways, illustrating context-dependent breadth of regulation in Medicago.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Off-target transactivation; broader flavonoid remodeling; possible metabolic burden.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ANR; ANS; LAR; FLS; UGT72L1; putative TT8 homologs; TTG1 ortholog; weakly induced transporter-related genes including TT12/TT19/AHA10-like genes
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions with TT8 and TTG1 in the Arabidopsis MBW complex; analogous complex implied here
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates multiple PA/flavonoid biosynthetic and associated genes, especially ANR and UGT72L1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TT2 expression was sufficient to induce massive PA accumulation in Medicago hairy roots. 2. Transcript profiling identified broad activation of PA/flavonoid genes and candidate downstream functions. 3. TT2 therefore behaves as a dominant regulatory switch for PA metabolism in this heterologous legume context.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Seed coat naturally; hairy roots after transgenic activation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and protective polyphenol; forage quality and health-related functions
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Transgenic constitutive expression in this study; native upstream developmental inputs not analyzed.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoters/endogenous genes; not directly transformed in this study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Likely useful co-regulator for stable PA engineering with TT2. Stacking MYB-bHLH-WD40 components may improve robustness and tissue specificity of PA induction.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Two putative TT8 homologs were moderately induced by TT2, suggesting reinforcement of the PA regulatory complex.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive positive regulator within the PA-specific MBW complex rather than sole determinant in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TT2-expressing hairy roots; seed-coat PA developmental context.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: TT2-induced in hairy roots; homologous role inferred in seed coat.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Arabidopsis TT8 is required for expression of DFR and BAN genes in siliques/seed coat.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional control of PA biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as putative homologs, not fully functionally characterized in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Unknown; could also affect anthocyanin-related programs due to shared MBW circuitry.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Likely late flavonoid/PA genes such as ANR and other branch enzymes, by analogy to Arabidopsis
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely component of MYB-bHLH-WD40 complex with TT2 and TTG1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely activates late flavonoid/PA biosynthetic genes in partnership with TT2
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Two TT8-like bHLH genes were induced by TT2 in hairy roots. 2. Their behavior supports the existence of a conserved MBW regulatory module in Medicago PA metabolism. 3. They are likely supportive co-regulators of PA gene activation.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Induced downstream of ectopic TT2 in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter/endogenous gene; not directly transformed in this study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate cofactor for PA engineering; combining WD40 scaffold with TT2/bHLH components may improve complex formation and trait stability.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A TTG1 ortholog was induced by TT2 and showed developmental expression similar to PA biosynthetic genes, supporting a role in PA regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Affymetrix microarray (2.3-fold induction); developmental expression analysis from gene atlas.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Likely required scaffold/co-regulator of the PA regulatory complex, though not individually tested here.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TT2-expressing hairy roots; developing seed coat.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: TT2-induced in hairy roots; developmental kinetics similar to ANR and CHS in seed coat.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Arabidopsis TTG1 regulates trichome differentiation and anthocyanin/PA biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional regulation of PA/flavonoid genes through regulatory complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Affymetrix microarray; gene atlas developmental expression data.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as one of the developmental coexpression markers supporting seed-coat PA-related candidates.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May participate in additional TTG1-dependent developmental pathways beyond PA metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Likely late flavonoid/PA genes as part of MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely WD40 component of MYB-bHLH-WD40 regulatory complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports activation of PA/flavonoid biosynthetic genes in complex with TT2 and TT8-like bHLHs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A Medicago TTG1 ortholog was induced by TT2 in hairy roots. 2. Its seed developmental expression paralleled PA marker genes. 3. This supports a conserved MBW complex controlling PA metabolism in Medicago.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Induced downstream of ectopic TT2 in this study.
